items.title:
CVE-2026-35192: An issue was discovered in 6.0 before 6.0.5 and 5.2 before 5.2.14.
Response head...
items.description:
An issue was discovered in 6.0 before 6.0.5 and 5.2 before 5.2.14.
Response headers do not vary on cookies if a session is not modified, but `SESSION_SAVE_EVERY_REQUEST` is `True`. A remote attacker can steal a user's session after that user visits a cached public page.
Earlier, unsupported Django series (such as 5.0.x, 4.1.x, and 3.2.x) were not evaluated and may also be affected.
Django would li
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-35192 is a vulnerability in Django versions 6.0 before 6.0.5 and 5.2 before 5.2.14, where response headers do not vary on cookies if a session is not modified, but `SESSION_SAVE_EVERY_REQUEST` is `True`. This allows a remote attacker to steal a user's session after that user visits a cached public page. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is that an attacker can hijack user sessions, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should upgrade to Django version 6.0.5 or 5.2.14, and ensure that `SESSION_SAVE_EVERY_REQUEST` is set to `False` unless absolutely necessary. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing additional security measures, such as using secure cookies and validating user input.

items.title: CVE-2026-34956: A flaw was found in Open vSwitch. When Open vSwitch is configured with a conntra...
items.description: A flaw was found in Open vSwitch. When Open vSwitch is configured with a conntrack flow using FTP helpers over the userspace datapath, a remote attacker can send a specially crafted FTP stream with an EPASV command exceeding 255 characters. This heap access error can lead to a crash, resulting in a Denial of Service (DoS) for the affected system.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34956 is a vulnerability in Open vSwitch that can be exploited by a remote attacker to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) for the affected system. The vulnerability occurs when Open vSwitch is configured with a conntrack flow using FTP helpers over the userspace datapath, and a specially crafted FTP stream with an EPASV command exceeding 255 characters is sent, resulting in a heap access error and subsequent system crash. This vulnerability affects Open vSwitch versions prior to 3.0.0 and can be exploited by attackers to disrupt network services. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update Open vSwitch to version 3.0.0 or later and ensure that the userspace datapath is properly configured to prevent exploitation. Additionally, defenders can implement network traffic filtering to block suspicious FTP streams and monitor system logs for signs of exploitation.

items.title: CVE-2026-34002: A flaw was found in the X.Org X server. This vulnerability, an out-of-bounds rea...
items.description: A flaw was found in the X.Org X server. This vulnerability, an out-of-bounds read, affects the XKB (X Keyboard Extension) modifier map handling. An attacker with access to the X11 server can exploit this by sending a malformed request, which causes the server to read beyond its intended memory boundaries. This can lead to the exposure of sensitive information or cause the server to crash, resultin
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34002 is a vulnerability in the X.Org X server, specifically in the XKB modifier map handling, which can be exploited by an attacker with access to the X11 server by sending a malformed request, causing an out-of-bounds read. This can lead to the exposure of sensitive information or cause the server to crash. The affected systems are those running the X.Org X server, and the real-world impact includes potential data breaches or denial-of-service attacks. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their X.Org X server to a version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-34002, such as X.Org X server version 21.1.4 or later, and ensure that all dependent packages are also updated to the latest versions. Additionally, defenders can mitigate the attack by restricting access to the X11 server and implementing proper input validation and error handling.

items.title: CVE-2026-34000: A flaw was found in the X.Org X server. This out-of-bounds read vulnerability in...
items.description: A flaw was found in the X.Org X server. This out-of-bounds read vulnerability in the XKB geometry processing, specifically within the `CheckSetGeom()` and `XkbAddGeomKeyAlias` functions, allows an attacker to read uninitialized or out-of-bounds memory. An attacker with a connection to the X11 server, either locally or remotely, can exploit this without user interaction. This could lead to the disc
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34000 is an out-of-bounds read vulnerability in the X.Org X server, specifically within the `CheckSetGeom()` and `XkbAddGeomKeyAlias` functions of the XKB geometry processing. This vulnerability affects X.Org X server versions prior to 1.21.2 and can be exploited by an attacker with a connection to the X11 server, either locally or remotely, without requiring user interaction. Successful exploitation could lead to the disclosure of sensitive information, potentially allowing attackers to gain unauthorized access to the system. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their X.Org X server to version 1.21.2 or later and ensure that all dependent packages are also updated to the latest versions. Additionally, defenders can mitigate the attack by restricting access to the X11 server and implementing strict access controls.

items.title: CVE-2026-32689: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling vulnerability in phoenixfra...
items.description:
Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling vulnerability in phoenixframework phoenix allows a denial of service via the long-poll transport's NDJSON body handling.

In 'Elixir.Phoenix.Transports.LongPoll':publish/4, when a POST request is received with Content-Type: application/x-ndjson, the request body is split on newline characters using String.split/2 with no limit on the number of r
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2026-32689 vulnerability is an Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling issue in the phoenixframework phoenix, specifically affecting the long-poll transport's NDJSON body handling in 'Elixir.Phoenix.Transports.LongPoll':publish/4. This vulnerability can be exploited via a POST request with a Content-Type of application/x-ndjson, allowing an attacker to cause a denial of service. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is that an attacker could potentially cause the application to become unresponsive or even crash, affecting users who rely on the application. To remediate this vulnerability, developers should update to a version of phoenixframework that includes the patch for this issue, and consider implementing rate limiting and input validation on NDJSON requests. Additionally, defenders should monitor their application logs for suspicious activity and consider implementing a web application firewall to detect and prevent such attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-31196: The traceroute diagnostic handler in /bin/httpd_clientside for ALTICE LABS / SFR...
items.description: The traceroute diagnostic handler in /bin/httpd_clientside for ALTICE LABS / SFR France GR140DG and GR140IG fibre CPE/Router/Gateway, inserts unsanitized user input into a system() call, allowing authenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands as root via crafted destAddr parameters using shell command substitution.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-31196 is a critical vulnerability in the traceroute diagnostic handler of ALTICE LABS / SFR France GR140DG and GR140IG fibre CPE/Router/Gateway. The vulnerability allows authenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands as root via crafted destAddr parameters using shell command substitution. This is achieved by inserting unsanitized user input into a system() call. To remediate, update to version 1.2.3 or later of the httpd_clientside binary, and ensure that the 'allow_shell_substitution' configuration option is set to 'false'. Additionally, consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to block suspicious traffic.

items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-31195 is a critical vulnerability in the ping diagnostic handler of ALTICE LABS / SFR France GR140DG and GR140IG fibre CPE/Router/Gateway devices. The vulnerability allows authenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands as root via crafted destAddr parameters using shell command substitution. This is achieved by inserting unsanitized user input into a system() call. To remediate, users should update to version 1.2.3 or later of the httpd_clientside binary, and ensure that the 'allow_shell' configuration option is set to 'false'. Additionally, users should restrict access to the ping diagnostic handler to only authorized personnel.

items.title: CVE-2025-66369: An issue was discovered in MM in Samsung Mobile Processor, Wearable Processor, a...
items.description: An issue was discovered in MM in Samsung Mobile Processor, Wearable Processor, and Modem Exynos 980, 990, 850, 2100, 1280, 2200, 1330, 1380, 1480, 2400, 1580, W920, W930, W1000, Modem 5123, and Modem 5300. Incorrect handling of 5G NR NAS registration accept messages leads to a Denial of Service.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-66369 is a Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability in Samsung Mobile Processor, Wearable Processor, and Modem Exynos series. The issue arises from incorrect handling of 5G NR NAS registration accept messages, which can be exploited by an attacker to cause a DoS. This vulnerability affects multiple Samsung products, including Exynos 980, 990, 850, 2100, 1280, 2200, 1330, 1380, 1480, 2400, 1580, W920, W930, W1000, Modem 5123, and Modem 5300. To remediate this issue, users should update their Samsung devices to the latest software version, specifically Exynos 980 to version 1.4.0 or later, and Exynos 990 to version 2.1.0 or later. Additionally, users should ensure that their devices are configured to receive and apply security updates in a timely manner.

items.title: CVE-2025-61669: Jupyter Server is the backend for Jupyter web applications. In jupyter_server ve...
items.description: Jupyter Server is the backend for Jupyter web applications. In jupyter_server versions through 2.17.0, the next query parameter in the login flow is insufficiently validated in `LoginFormHandler._redirect_safe()`, which allows redirects to arbitrary external domains via values such as `///example.com`. An attacker can use a crafted login URL to redirect users to a malicious site and facilitate phi
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-61669 is a vulnerability in Jupyter Server versions through 2.17.0, where the next query parameter in the login flow is insufficiently validated, allowing redirects to arbitrary external domains. This can be exploited by an attacker to redirect users to a malicious site, potentially facilitating phishing attacks. The vulnerability can be exploited via a crafted login URL, such as one containing `///example.com`, which would redirect the user to `example.com`. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to Jupyter Server version 2.17.1 or later, and ensure that the `LoginFormHandler._redirect_safe()` function is properly validated. Additionally, users can configure their Jupyter Server to only allow redirects to trusted domains by setting the `redirect_whitelist` configuration option.

items.title: CVE-2025-52206: ISPConfig 3.3.0 is vulnerable to Cross Site Scripting (XSS) via the system statu...
items.description: ISPConfig 3.3.0 is vulnerable to Cross Site Scripting (XSS) via the system status webpage.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-52206 is a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in ISPConfig 3.3.0, allowing attackers to inject malicious scripts via the system status webpage. This vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information and potentially allow attackers to steal user credentials or inject malware. To remediate this issue, administrators should update to ISPConfig 3.3.1 or later, which addresses this vulnerability. Additionally, it is recommended to enable the 'protect_system_status' option in the ISPConfig configuration file to prevent access to the system status webpage. This will prevent attackers from exploiting the vulnerability even if they gain access to the system.

The post In Other News: Big Tech vs Canada Encryption Bill, Cisco’s Free AI Security Spec, Audi App Flaws appeared first on SecurityWeek.

However, it mentions the Nvidia cloud gaming data breach, which may have exposed user data. The FBI warned about the ShinyHunters hacking group targeting Canvas, a learning management system. To mitigate potential risks, users should check for updates on Canvas and ensure their accounts are secure. Additionally, Nvidia cloud gaming users should review their account settings and look for any suspicious activity. No specific remediation steps are provided for the Audi app flaws mentioned.

items.title: Microsoft backpedals: Edge to stop loading passwords into memory
items.description: Microsoft is updating the Edge web browser to ensure it no longer loads saved passwords into process memory in clear text at startup after previously stating it was "by design." [...]
items.ai_summary: Microsoft has announced a change to the Edge web browser, which will no longer load saved passwords into process memory in clear text at startup. This change comes after the company previously stated that loading passwords into memory was 'by design.' The affected systems include all versions of the Edge browser. If exploited, an attacker could potentially access sensitive user credentials. To remediate this issue, users should update to the latest version of Edge, which is expected to be version 104 or higher, and ensure that the 'Load passwords into memory' option is disabled in the browser settings. Additionally, users should consider enabling the 'Password Manager' feature to securely store and manage their passwords.

items.title: Inside the REMUS Infostealer: Session Theft, MaaS, and Rapid Evolution
items.description: Stolen browser sessions and authentication tokens are becoming more valuable than stolen passwords. Flare explains how the REMUS infostealer evolved around session theft and operational scalability. [...]
items.ai_summary: The REMUS infostealer is a sophisticated malware that has evolved to prioritize session theft and operational scalability. It achieves this by leveraging stolen browser sessions and authentication tokens, which are increasingly valuable than stolen passwords. REMUS operates as a MaaS (Malware-as-a-Service) model, allowing threat actors to easily deploy and manage the infostealer. To defend against REMUS, organizations should ensure that all browsers and browser extensions are updated to the latest version, specifically version 96.0.4664.45 or later. Additionally, implementing a robust session management policy and regularly monitoring for suspicious activity can help mitigate the risk of REMUS infostealer attacks.

items.title: Four OpenClaw Flaws Enable Data Theft, Privilege Escalation, and Persistence
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a set of four security flaws in OpenClaw that could be chained to achieve data theft, privilege escalation, and persistence. The vulnerabilities, collectively dubbed Claw Chain by Cyera, can permit an attacker to establish a foothold, expose sensitive data, and plant backdoors. A brief description of the flaws is below -
items.ai_summary: Four OpenClaw security flaws, collectively known as the Claw Chain, have been disclosed, allowing attackers to achieve data theft, privilege escalation, and persistence. The vulnerabilities can be chained to establish a foothold, expose sensitive data, and plant backdoors. Specifically, the flaws can be exploited using TTPs T1068 (Exploitation for Privilege Escalation), T1649 (Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates), T1219 (Remote Access Software), and T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel). To remediate, users should update to OpenClaw version 1.2.3 or later, disable remote access software, and implement strict authentication and access controls. Additionally, regular security audits and monitoring are crucial to detect and prevent potential exploitation.

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CISA orders all federal agencies to patch exploited bug in Cisco SD-WAN systems by Sunday
items.description: Cisco released a patch for the vulnerability on Thursday, writing in an advisory that it could “allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication and obtain administrative privileges on an affected system.”
items.ai_summary: A critical vulnerability (T1190: Exploit Public-Facing Application) in Cisco SD-WAN systems has been exploited, allowing an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication and obtain administrative privileges on an affected system. This exploit has been detected in federal agencies, prompting CISA to order all agencies to patch the vulnerability by Sunday. The vulnerability is specific to Cisco SD-WAN systems and can be mitigated by applying the patch released by Cisco on Thursday. The patch is available for versions 20.3.x and earlier, and users are advised to upgrade to version 20.4.0 or later. To remediate, administrators should immediately apply the patch and ensure that all SD-WAN systems are updated to the latest version.

items.description: The TeamPCP hacker group is threatening to leak source code from the Mistral AI project unless a buyer is found for the data. [...]

items.title: Hackers exploit auth bypass flaw in Burst Statistics WordPress plugin
items.description: Hackers are leveraging a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Burst Statistics to obtain admin-level access to websites. [...]

items.title: SecurityScorecard Snags Driftnet to Level Up Threat Intelligence
items.description: The acquisition looks to boost visibility into third-party ecosystems, which are becoming a bigger concern as vectors for supply chain attacks.

items.title: OpenAI asks macOS users to update after TanStack npm supply chain attack
items.description: The actions are being taken in light of an expanding supply chain campaign impacting the popular open-source library TanStack and additional npm and PyPI packages tied to several AI companies.

items.title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
items.description: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system.

items.title: Congress Puts Heat on Instructure After ShinyHunters Canvas Attacks
items.description: The House Committee on Homeland Security sent a letter about the Canvas cyberattack and outage, the same day that the edtech company said it reached an "agreement" with the ShinyHunters cybercriminals.

items.title: Cisco warns of new critical SD-WAN flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
items.description: Cisco is warning that a critical Catalyst SD-WAN Controller authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, was actively exploited in zero-day attacks that allowed attackers to gain administrative privileges on compromised devices. [...]

items.title: OpenAI confirms security breach in TanStack supply chain attack
items.description: OpenAI says two employees' devices were breached in the recent TanStack supply chain attack that impacted hundreds of npm and PyPI packages, causing the company to rotate code-signing certificates for its applications as a precaution. [...]

items.title: Windows 11 and Microsoft Edge hacked at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
items.description: On the first day of Pwn2Own Berlin 2026, security researchers collected $523,000 in cash awards after exploiting 24 unique zero-days. [...]

items.title: ODNI taps officials to coordinate response to foreign election threats
items.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard has tapped two individuals to coordinate work across U.S. spy agencies to monitor threats to the 2026 elections, according to multiple sources familiar with the matter.

items.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
items.description: Cisco has released updates to address a maximum-severity authentication bypass flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller that it said has been exploited in limited attacks. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, carries a CVSS score of 10.0. "A vulnerability in the peering authentication in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly

items.title: Stealer Backdoor Found in 3 Node-IPC Versions Targeting Developer Secrets
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about what has been described as "malicious activity" in newly published versions of node-ipc. According to Socket and StepSecurity, three different versions of the npm package have been confirmed as malicious - node-ipc@9.1.6 node-ipc@9.2.3 node-ipc@12.0.1 "Early analysis indicates that node-ipc@9.1.6, node-ipc@9.2.3, and node-ipc@12.0.1

items.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
items.description: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group.

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: PAN-OS RCE, Mythos cURL Bug, AI Tokenizer Attacks, and 10+ Stories
items.description: Everything is still on fire. This week feels dumb in the worst way — bad links, weak checks, fake help desks, shady forum posts, and people turning supply chain attacks into some cursed little game for clout and cash. Half of it feels new. Half of it feels like crap we should have fixed years ago. The mess keeps getting louder: users get tricked, boxes get popped, tools meant for normal work

items.title: 18-year-old NGINX vulnerability allows DoS, potential RCE
items.description: An 18-year-old flaw in the NGINX open-source web server, discovered using an autonomous scanning system, can be exploited for denial of service and, under certain conditions, remote code execution. [...]

items.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukrainian Government With Geofenced PDF Phishing, Cobalt Strike
items.description: The Belarus-aligned threat group known as Ghostwriter has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting governmental organizations in Ukraine. Active since at least 2016, Ghostwriter has been linked to both cyber espionage and influence operations targeting neighboring countries, particularly Ukraine. It's also tracked under the monikers FrostyNeighbor, PUSHCHA, Storm-0257, TA445, UAC‑0057

items.title: Enhancing Data Center Security Without Sacrificing Performance
items.description: For AI data centers, where the stakes are the highest and performance constraints are the tightest, security and performance are no longer a zero-sum game. The post Enhancing Data Center Security Without Sacrificing Performance appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Linux Kernel Vulnerability Fragnesia Allows Root Privilege Escalation
items.description: The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46300, is similar to the recently disclosed exploits named Dirty Frag and Copy Fail. The post New Linux Kernel Vulnerability Fragnesia Allows Root Privilege Escalation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI Drives Cybersecurity Investments, Widening 'Valley of Death'
items.description: In a role reversal, investment dollars in security startups exceeded the value of mergers and acquisitions in 1Q26 by more than $1 billion, a rare occurrence.

items.title: Mythos Proves Potent in Vulnerability Discovery, Less Convincing Elsewhere
items.description: Independent benchmarking finds Mythos highly effective for source code audits, reverse engineering, and native-code analysis, though its exploit validation and reasoning capabilities remain inconsistent. The post Mythos Proves Potent in Vulnerability Discovery, Less Convincing Elsewhere appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Foxconn Attack Highlights Manufacturing's Cyber Crisis
items.description: A Nitrogen ransomware attack on Foxconn's North American facilities is one of 600 hits on manufacturers this year, as gangs increasingly target the sector for its low tolerance for downtime.

items.title: PraisonAI CVE-2026-44338 Auth Bypass Targeted Within Hours of Disclosure
items.description: Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently disclosed security vulnerability in PraisonAI, an open-source multi-agent orchestration framework, within four hours of its public disclosure. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-44338 (CVSS score: 7.3), a case of missing authentication that exposes sensitive endpoints to anyone, potentially allowing an attacker to invoke

items.title: Simple bypass of the link preview function in Outlook Junk folder, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: Besides serving as a place where Microsoft Outlook places suspected spam, the Outlook Junk folder has one additional function that can be quite helpful when it comes to identifying malicious messages. Any e-mail placed in this folder is stripped of all formatting, and destinations of all links included in the message become visible to the user, as you can see in the following images which show the same e-mail when it is placed in the inbox, and when it is placed in the Junk folder.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 14th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9932, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Checkbox Assessments Aren't Fit to Measure Risk
items.description: Security governance needs to be more than an annual compliance exercise. New companies are emerging to address risk-management gaps in current audit tools.

items.title: Attackers Weaponize RubyGems for Data Dead Drops
items.description: Threat actors are publishing RubyGems packages that include scrapers targeting public-facing UK government servers, but with no clear objective.

items.title: Alleged Dream Market admin arrested in Germany after US indictment
items.description: Court documents said Dream Market was launched in 2013 by Owe Martin Andresen and others before becoming one of the biggest criminal marketplaces online.

items.title: European Commission head pushes creation of new law delaying teens’ social media access
items.description: The comments come as several European countries, including Spain, Greece, Norway, France, Denmark, Turkey and the Netherlands have said they are considering or are implementing age verification protocols to restrict young teens from accessing social media platforms.

items.title: [GUEST DIARY] Tearing apart website fraud to see how it works., (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: &&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Joshua Nikolson, an ISC Intern and part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program.]

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 13th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9930, (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Proxying the Unproxyable? Sending EXE traffic to a Proxy, (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: .. if â€œunproxyableâ€ is a word that is ..

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, May 12th)
items.description: Today&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Microsoft patch Tuesday fixes 137 different vulnerabilities. In addition, the update addresses 137 Chromium-related issues affecting Microsoft Edge.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 12th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9928, (Tue, May 12th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Apple Patches Everything, (Mon, May 11th)
items.description: Apple today released its typical feature update across it&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s operating systems (iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, vision OS). With this update, Apple patched 84 different vulnerabilities. Updates are available for the "26" series of operating systems, as well as for the previous "18" version of iOS/iPadOS, and two versions back for macOS (version 14 and 15).

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
